question: When I roughly know a Stellar docs page is called State Archival, what is the fastest precise way to find its canonical page before reading it?

golden.answer: Use search_doc_titles with the expected page or section title words. Inspect the returned breadcrumb and url_without_anchor to choose the canonical page, then follow with get_doc_page_sections or a content-bearing docs search. Title search is deliberately heading-focused and should not be treated as full-body research.
keyFacts: Uses title-only search to locate pages or headings by their expected name.
Follows the selected url_without_anchor with a page-reading operation.
avoid: Do NOT claim title hits contain the complete page body.
Do NOT choose a result solely from a highlighted word without checking its breadcrumb and path.
golden.notes: Live docs title-discovery behavior; exact hit ordering may drift.

sources.note: Fetch-verified local path exists and defines the exercised contract/workflow.

evidence: 2026-07-11 localhost:8787 search→execute probe observed: HTTP 200; search_doc_titles returned envelope ok=true with title/heading hits for State Archival.
specs/stellar-docs.json exists and supports the behavioral golden.
Sibling sweep: reviewed existing battery cases sharing the exercised surface; no contradictory behavioral golden found.
